text: We start our dissection by defining the right upper lobe bronchus and dissecting and removing the level 10 lymph nodes between the right upper lobe bronchi and the bronchus intermedius.
We proceed with our dissection going around the hilum and opening the pleura around the right upper lobe.
The lymph node package sitting on top of the anterior truncus of the pulmonary artery is completely dissected.
Next, the superior pulmonary vein is completely defined.
The space between the superior vein and the middle lobe vein is dissected.
The pulmonary vein is dissected circumferentially and is stapled.
we often place a silk suture around the vein to facilitate the placement of the stapler.
The lymph node package around the truncus anterior is dissected.
The truncus is dissected free and isolated.
The truncus anterior is dissected circumferentially.
A silk suture is placed around it
and it is stapled and divided.
The posterior ascending segmental artery is identified.
The peribronchial nodes separating the segmental artery from the bronchus are dissected and removed.
The posterior ascending segmental artery is circumferentially isolated.
A silk suture is placed around it and it is stapled and divided.
At this point, the bronchus should be completely free.
A staple is placed around it and the bronchus is stapled and divided.
At this point, all hilar structures have been divided.
A linear stapler is used to complete the fissure going from anterior to posterior.
The right upper lobe is introduced into a specimen bag and removed from the thoracic cavity.
After port placement and a subcarinal node dissection, the right upper lobe and the bronchus intermedius are defined.
The fissure is then open and the pulmonary artery clearly identified.
The fissure is then completed posteriorly.
Because this was a well-defined fissure, we did not use staplers.
The recurrent ascending branch of the pulmonary artery is identified and clearly dissected.
We then proceeded to define the fissure anteriorly.
In this case, the fissure anteriorly was not well defined.
The pulmonary vein is identified and the space between the branch to the upper lobe and middle lobe clearly dissected.
At this point, the anterior part of the fissure can be safely stapled.
The posterior ascending segmental artery is circumferentially dissected, stapled and divided.
The bronchus is then circumferentially dissected and it is divided with a linear stapler.
Level 10 peribronchial nodes are removed from around the truncus anterior.
At this point, the truncus anterior is divided with the linear stapler.
The last structure in the hilum is the vein, which is divided with the stapler.
The right upper lobe is then placed into a specimen bag and removed.
Prior to undocking the robot, superior mediastinal node dissection is performed.
